Title: Welcome. Let's Start Growing.
Subtitle: Peaks and Valleys Exercise
Summary: Establishes the journey of faith and uses personal reflection to see God's hand in one's personal history.

Title: Salvation & Baptism
Subtitle: ABCs of the Gospel (Admit Believe Confess)
Tool: ABCs of the Gospel (Admit Believe Confess)
Summary: Teaches the Roman Road (Admit, Believe, Confess), identifies baptism as the outward sign of an inward faith, and addresses the assurance of salvation.

Title: Prayer
Subtitle: Lord's Prayer pattern and prayer tool
Tool: Lord's Prayer pattern and prayer tool
Summary: Provides a 6-step relational pattern for prayer using the Lord's Prayer: connecting with the Father, worshiping His name, praying His agenda, dependence, forgiveness, and spiritual warfare.

Title: The Bible
Subtitle: Daily Bible Reading app
Summary: Defines Scripture as God-breathed and introduces the 3-question discovery method: what it says about God, what it says about man, and what you will do or believe.

Title: The Church
Subtitle: Acts 2:42 four pillars
Summary: Defines the church's purpose to love, equip, and serve based on the Acts 2:42-47 model of devoted fellowship and intentional community.

Title: Sharing Jesus with Others
Subtitle: Gospel diagram and BLESS rhythm
Summary: Something amazing just happened in your life - and the most natural thing in the world is to tell someone about it. Introduces the heart of evangelism and a simplified version of the BLESS rhythm (Begin with Prayer, Listen, Eat, Serve, Story). A deeper dive into BLESS comes in 203.

Title: A Life of Repentance
Subtitle: Repentance Rhythm cycle
Summary: Frames repentance as an ongoing rhythm of the heart - turning from sin and back to God - that keeps your faith alive and your walk with Jesus moving forward.

Title: Loving God
Subtitle: Great Commandment Diagram
Summary: Explores the Greatest Commandment - loving God with all your heart, soul, mind, and strength - while also revealing the character and nature of God as Father. Introduces the Trinity (Father, Son, Spirit) as the foundation for lessons 1-3.

Title: New Identity in Jesus
Subtitle: Clothed in Christ through the Great Exchange
Summary: John 1 reveals Jesus as the eternal Word and true Light who came into the world. Whoever receives Him is given the right to become a child of God. Through the Great Exchange, Jesus takes your sin, gives you His righteousness, clothes you with Himself, and makes you a new creation.

Title: The Guiding of the Holy Spirit
Subtitle: The Five Actions of the Holy Spirit
Tool: The Five Actions of the Holy Spirit
Summary: The Holy Spirit can feel mysterious, but Jesus promised Him as a Helper who is with you forever. From the pillar of cloud and fire that led Israel through the wilderness to the Spirit who lives inside every believer today, God has always been a guide to His people. This lesson walks through what the Spirit actually does in your life and why you can trust Him to lead you every step of the way.

Title: Spiritual Disciplines - Abiding in Christ
Subtitle: Daily Prayers tool
Summary: Building sustainable rhythms of prayer, Scripture, fellowship, stewardship, and fighting sin as ways of staying connected to Jesus. Includes practical guidance on daily devotional habits - Bible reading, prayer, journaling, and Bible memory - as the daily rhythm that keeps the disciplines alive.

Title: Overview of the Bible
Subtitle: OT and NT Bookshelf walkthrough
Story: Josiah Finding the Book of the Law (2 Kings 22)
Summary: Welcome to a guided tour of the most important book you will ever read. The Bible isn't a single book. It is a library of 66 unique volumes written by 40 authors across 1,500 years. Learning how this life-changing book is organized will help you understand it and remember how the story of God fits together. First of three connected Bible lessons (map - road trip - skill).

Title: Biblical Storyline
Subtitle: Four Parts (Creation / Fall / Redemption / Restoration) + BibleProject link
Tool: Four Parts (Creation / Fall / Redemption / Restoration) + BibleProject link
Summary: Creation, Fall, Redemption, Restoration - the four-part story of the Bible with Jesus at the center. Second of three connected Bible lessons (map - road trip - skill).

Title: How to Study the Bible
Subtitle: OIA method (Observe / Interpret / Apply) with Psalm 23 practice passage
Tool: OIA method (Observe / Interpret / Apply) with Psalm 23 practice passage
Summary: Builds on the Three Discovery Questions from 101.04 with a deeper inductive method. Teaches OIA (Observe, Interpret, Apply) with a guided practice on Psalm 23. Third of three connected Bible lessons (map - road trip - skill).

Title: The Grace and Mercy Cycle
Subtitle: The Grace and Mercy Cycle
Story: The Boy and the Fruit Tree
Summary: Four words explain what God did for you at the cross: Grace, Mercy, Justified, and Righteous. You will see how these four realities work together in a cycle that starts with God's love and flows through you to others.

Title: Living the Fruit of the Spirit
Subtitle: Fruit of the Spirit (9 fruits visual)
Tool: Fruit of the Spirit (9 fruits visual)
Summary: Sanctification is the process - the Fruit of the Spirit is the evidence. Explores how God progressively conforms you to the image of Christ through the Spirit's work, and how the nine fruits of Galatians 5 are the visible proof that the process is happening. Know who you are, then live out who you are.

Title: Repentance and the Learning Circle
Subtitle: The Learning Circle (interactive)
Summary: Everyone drifts - into obvious sin like the younger son, or into pride like the older one. Both ditched the Father. Both needed to turn back. This lesson uses the full Prodigal Son story and The Learning Circle tool to teach the ongoing rhythm of repentance and belief that marks the whole Christian life, from noticing the drift through godly grief to practical planning and obedience.

Title: Loving Your Neighbors
Subtitle: Expanding Circles (home church neighborhood world)
Summary: Explores the second great commandment - love your neighbor as yourself, love one another as I have loved you, the golden rule, and considering others better than yourself.

Title: Loving Your Family at Home - Your First Neighbors
Subtitle: Weekly connection app
Story: The Forgiveness Loop (Examine. Confess. Forgive. Rebuild) OR The 3 Chairs of Family Life
Summary: Establishing a gospel-centered home where your family is your first mission field - and where forgiveness is most needed and most tested. Covers marriage, singleness, household life as discipleship, and the hard work of forgiving the people closest to you.

Title: Loving Your Church Family
Subtitle: One Anothers of the NT
Summary: Living out the New Testament one another commands - serving in the church, caring for each other, resolving conflict, and what it looks like to truly belong to a spiritual family.

Title: Discovering Your Gifts - APEST - Equip the saints for the work of ministry
Subtitle: Spiritual gifts assessment
Summary: Discovering your unique capacity for serving the body of Christ.

Title: Suffering
Subtitle: Verse card: James 1:2-4
Summary: A biblical framework for when life gets hard - understanding that trials produce endurance, and that God is with us in the valley, not just on the mountaintop.

Title: Worship and the Glory of God
Subtitle: Worship Is... (Piper quote + John 4:23)
Summary: Worship is gladly reflecting back to God the radiance of His worth. This lesson explores what it means to worship God in spirit and truth - not just in a Sunday service but as the posture of your whole life.

Title: Giving & Generosity
Subtitle: Generosity Ladder
Summary: In this lesson you will explore why generosity and tithing flows from gratitude, not obligation, and why giving is the clearest thermometer of where your heart actually is. Knowing that every thing is God's and you are His steward breaks the grip of money on our lives and will bring you lasting financial freedom.

Title: Fighting for Purity
Subtitle: FIGHT acrostic for purity
Summary: Honoring God with our bodies and minds as a response to the grace we have received - practical strategies for fighting temptation.

Title: Baptism & the Lord's Supper
Subtitle: Two Ordinances side by side
Summary: The meaning and beauty of Baptism and the Lord's Supper as symbols of the gospel - and how the Lord's Supper points forward to the return of Christ.

Title: The Great Commission - Your Personal Ministry
Subtitle: Acts 1:8 concentric circles
Summary: The last words of Jesus and what they mean for every believer: you are sent. Great Commission vision with Acts 1:8 concentric circles and the home as first mission field.

Title: BLESS - Begin in Prayer
Subtitle: BLESS rhythm and tracker
Summary: The deeper dive into the BLESS rhythm introduced in 101.06. Five practical rhythms for living on mission where you live, work, and play: Begin with Prayer, Listen, Eat, Serve, Story. This is the skill-training version of what 101 introduced as heart preparation.

Title: BLESS - Listen and Eat - Show Hospitality
Subtitle: The Open Table (hospitality IS vs ISN'T)
Tool: The Open Table (hospitality IS vs ISN'T)
Summary: Life Skill: Using your home and your table to welcome strangers into the family of God - why hospitality is not entertaining but mission.

Title: BLESS - Serve with Loving Sacrifice
Subtitle: Servant Leadership pattern (came not to be served but to serve)
Tool: Servant Leadership pattern (came not to be served but to serve)
Summary: The Serve letter of BLESS. Jesus came not to be served but to serve, and His followers take the same posture. Practical ways to serve the people God has placed around you, with loving sacrifice as the shape of every act.

Title: BLESS - Share Your Story and the Gospel
Subtitle: Three Stories (theirs, yours, Jesus')
Summary: The second S of BLESS - the how of sharing faith. By the time the Begin, Listen, Eat, and Serve rhythms have done their work, the bridge of relationship is built, so talking about life and faith feels natural. Teaches the Three Stories pattern as one example of how to share: hear their story, share your story, share Jesus' story.

Title: Faith at Work and School - Your Second Space
Subtitle: 1st, 2nd, 3rd Spaces framework
Summary: Your life unfolds across three kinds of space. 1st Space is home and close friends. 2nd Space is work and school, where most of your waking hours go. 3rd Space is everywhere else: hobbies, strangers, parties, meet-ups. This lesson is about living faithfully in your 2nd Space, the place where most of your time and most of your testimony gets written.

Title: Spiritual Warfare and Putting on the Armor of God
Subtitle: Armor of God (Ephesians 6)
Summary: Protecting the mission from the schemes of the enemy through prayer and truth and community.

Title: How to Disciple Others
Subtitle: Navigators Wheel (Christ + 4 spokes)
Summary: The practice of life-on-life investment - how to intentionally walk alongside someone and help them grow in their faith.

Title: Your Calling - Putting It All Together
Subtitle: Your SHAPE (gifts heart abilities personality experience)
Tool: Your SHAPE (gifts heart abilities personality experience)
Summary: Putting it all together: finding your specific team and role at Neighborhood Church.

Title: Leaders Have Character, Integrity, and Accountability
Subtitle: Jethro and Moses (Exodus 18)
Summary: The foundation of leadership is who you are when no one is watching. Character, integrity, and accountability are not leadership techniques - they are the soil everything else grows from. Caught not taught.

Title: Leaders Have Followers: Let's Get Started
Summary: You are not leading in the abstract - someone is actually following you. This lesson introduces the language of Your Personal Ministry and gives you a practical framework for walking your first (or next) disciple through the basics of faith.

Title: Leaders Pray for Others
Summary: Intercessory prayer is the engine underneath everything a leader does. This is not a repeat of learning how to pray - this is learning to cover your people by name, to pray before hard conversations, and to lead from your knees before you lead from your mouth.

Title: Leaders Help Others Belong
Summary: Great leaders find joy in seeing others connected to community - even community the leader is not part of. This lesson explores how leaders build environments of belonging and help people find their people.

Title: Leaders Lead Groups Well
Summary: The practical dynamics of leading a small group or Bible study - how to guide discussion, create space for the Spirit to work, handle the person who talks too much, and draw out the person who never talks.

Title: Leaders Give Great Talks
Summary: How to prepare and deliver a simple teaching that helps real people meet God. This is not seminary homiletics - it is how to open the Scriptures with a group of 5 or 15 and make the Word come alive.

Title: Leaders Serve All the Time
Summary: Servant leadership is not a chapter in a book - it is the posture of your whole life. Jesus led by washing feet and you will too. This lesson challenges the leader to make serving the default setting of their leadership.

Title: Leaders Are Disciplined
Summary: By now you have learned to gather, lead, teach, and serve. Discipline is the unglamorous engine that keeps all of it from falling apart over time. This lesson addresses time management, consistency, follow-through, and the long obedience in the same direction.

Title: Leaders Lean Into Conflict for Peace
Summary: The lesson no one wants but every leader needs. Correction in love, difficult people, maintaining unity without avoiding truth, and the courage to have the hard conversation that everyone else is avoiding.

Title: Leaders Challenge Others and Build Personal Ministry in Others
Summary: The ultimate goal of leadership is to make yourself unnecessary. This lesson brings the series full circle - from your own character in lesson 1 to now building personal ministry into the people you have been leading. Spiritual multiplication, passing the baton, and sending others to do what has been done in them.
